[Agent]: Huge congrats on your new car—I'm Amit from Aspire Insurance, happy to help! [User]: I just bought a Maruti Suzuki Swift VXI 2026 (petrol, manual) and want the cheapest comprehensive cover with app-based claims and zero-depreciation if doable. Could you pull a tailored quote aiming for Premium ₹8,000 annually?
[Agent]: Congrats — for a new Maruti Swift VXI (petrol, manual) a comprehensive policy with zero-depreciation and app-based claims typically costs around ₹9,000–₹12,000/year; hitting ₹8,000 is tight but possible if you accept a higher voluntary deductible or a limited promotional discount. I’ll focus on ICICI Lombard (strong app claims and fast settlements) and compare options like raising the voluntary deductible or dropping non-essential add‑ons to chase that premium. Which RTO city or pincode will the car be registered in so I can pull a tailored quote?
[User]: I'm on a tight budget and aiming for Premium ₹8,000 annually for a comprehensive policy with zero-dep and app-driven claims. For Premium ₹8,000, what exact coverages, voluntary deductible and IDV would I get, plus any roadside assistance or key exclusions I should know about?
[Agent]: Hitting ₹8,000/year for a comprehensive with zero-dep and app-based claims is tight but possible if you accept trade-offs like a higher voluntary deductible or slightly lower IDV. Typically at ₹8,000 for your Maruti Swift VXI you’d see an IDV around ₹5.5–6.5 lakh, a voluntary deductible of about ₹10,000–₹25,000, cover for own-damage + third‑party + zero‑depreciation (often limited to 1–2 claims) and basic roadside assistance (towing ~25–50 km, fuel/tyre/battery help). Key exclusions to note: wear-and-tear/mechanical breakdowns, tyre-only damage unless due to an accident, consumables/accessories unless added, and claims from prohibited use (DUI/racing); many customers choose a higher voluntary deductible or short-term ICICI Lombard promos to hit this premium.

Can you pull it now and confirm I can receive an instant app-based e-policy?
[Agent]: Got it — I’ll pull a live New India quote for MH12AB1234, list exact add-on prices for OEM parts, engine/gearbox and electrical/electronics, and attach the policy wording for your review. New India typically issues an instant app-based e-policy after online payment; I’ll confirm that for your exact quote. Shall I proceed to fetch the live quote now?
[User]: Before you fetch the live New India quote for MH12AB1234, can you list exactly what each add‑on (OEM parts, engine/gearbox, electrical/electronics) covers — including any sub‑limits, exclusions or depreciation rules — so I can decide what to add to Premium ₹6,000?
[Agent]: I understand — before we pull the live quote, here’s a clear, compact rundown so you can choose what truly protects your family and budget (base premium ₹6,000).  
OEM‑parts add‑on: replaces damaged parts with factory/OEM parts (body panels, fitted components) instead of aftermarket; excludes wear & tear, consumables (tyres, battery, bulbs) and non‑listed items; depreciation on items like battery/tyres usually still applies unless zero‑dep wording says otherwise, and claims need OEM invoices.

Electrical/electronics add‑on: covers ECU, wiring harness, sensors, infotainment and other electrical assemblies damaged by insured perils; excludes batteries, bulbs, mobile accessories, software faults and manufacturing defects, and insurers may apply per‑item caps or depreciation on plastic/trim—shall I fetch the live New India quote for MH12AB1234 with exact add‑on prices and the full endorsement wording?
